rationale: Baseline (iter 0) NLPD=1.8178 with complete pooling. Units clearly have different slopes (unit 14 has strong negative, unit 15 positive). NCP to avoid funnel geometry.
Iter 1 NLPD=1.3091 (vs baseline 1.8178). Independent slopes/intercepts ignores any correlation. LKJ prior models that correlation, potentially improving shrinkage.
Iter 2 NLPD=1.3073 (correlated NCP). Adding Student-t to handle potential outliers/heavy tails. If nu->large, degenerates to normal.
Iter 3 NLPD=1.3073 (tied), Student-t showed data is Gaussian. Trying heteroscedasticity: units may have different residual variances. NCP for sigmas via lognormal.

notes: Correlated slopes+intercepts + unit-specific sigma + hierarchical quadratic term
rationale: Iter 4 NLPD=1.3055 (best). Small gains from heteroscedasticity. Adding hierarchical quadratic gamma_j ~ N(mu_gamma, sigma_gamma) with sigma_gamma ~ Exp(2) to allow per-unit curvature while regularizing toward linear.

notes: Independent slopes+intercepts+quadratic + unit-specific sigma, no LKJ
rationale: Iter 5 NLPD=1.2910 (best). Quadratic term helped significantly. Testing if removing LKJ (which gave only 0.002 gain in iter 2) keeps gains while simplifying. Also relaxing sigma_gamma to Exp(1) from Exp(2).
Iter 5 NLPD=1.2910 (best, LKJ on alpha/beta + independent gamma + unit sigma). Iter 6 without LKJ gave 1.2933 (worse). Trying 3D LKJ to also model alpha-gamma, beta-gamma correlations.

notes: 3D LKJ with tighter tau[3]~HalfNormal(0.5) + HalfNormal priors on tau[1,2]
rationale: Iter 7 NLPD=1.2839 (best). 3D LKJ with unit-specific sigma. Trying tighter priors: tau[3]~HN(0.5) since quadratic spread should be small relative to linear; tau[1,2]~HN(2) instead of Exp(1).

notes: 3D LKJ(4) instead of LKJ(2) + unit-specific sigma, iter7 Exp priors
rationale: Iter 7 NLPD=1.2839 (best). Iter 8 with tighter tau priors gave 1.2861 (worse). With only J=15 groups, 3x3 correlation matrix may be overfit by LKJ(2). LKJ(4) shrinks toward independence more strongly.

notes: Piecewise linear (hinge at x=0): different slopes for x<0 and x>0, 3D LKJ(2) + unit-specific sigma
rationale: Iters 8-9 both non-improving (2 consecutive). Best is iter 7 (1.2839). Trying piecewise linear as alternative to polynomial: captures asymmetric slopes without quadratic constraints. Same 3D LKJ structure.

notes: 3-segment piecewise linear (knots at -1,+1) via ramp basis, 4D LKJ + unit-specific sigma
rationale: Iter 10 NLPD=1.2738 (best, piecewise at x=0). Piecewise linear outperformed polynomial. Extending to 3 segments with knots at x=-1 and x=+1 for finer slope variation. Using ramp basis: f(x)=alpha+beta*x+delta1*max(x+1,0)+delta2*max(x-1,0).
Iter 10 NLPD=1.2738 (best). Iter 11 (3 segments) diverged. Adding xi_j per unit to allow heteroscedasticity that grows/shrinks with |x|. Prior mu_xi~N(0,0.5), sigma_xi~Exp(2) for regularization.

notes: Piecewise linear with learned shared knot k~N(0,1) + 3D LKJ + unit-specific sigma
rationale: 2 consecutive non-improving (iters 11-12). Best is iter 10 (1.2738, knot at x=0). Last attempt: learning the knot location k from data instead of fixing at 0. k~N(0,1) allows the break to shift up to ~1 unit.
